summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Celebrity Buzz Index application. Backend APIs working 100%, frontend UI functional with 1 critical bug found in profanity filter.

critical_bugs.issue: Profanity filter not working - banned team names like 'racist team' and 'nazi team' are being accepted
critical_bugs.impact: Users can create teams with offensive/racist names, violating content policy

integration_issues.issue: Frontend doesn't validate team names before sending to backend, relies on backend validation which is failing

passed_tests: Player count banner displays players/celebrities/transfer window correctly
Today's Top Celeb News shows 8 headlines with AI-generated content
How It Works section displays 5 steps including Brown Bread bonus
Points methodology modal shows Brown Bread Bonus explanation with skull emoji
Share modal contains WhatsApp, Facebook, and X/Twitter sharing options
Top Picked Celebrities section appears in sidebar with celebrity rankings
Category selection loads celebrities correctly (tested Musicians category)
Add to team functionality updates budget correctly (£50M to £31M)
Leaderboard displays teams with points and rankings
Search functionality with Wikipedia autocomplete working
A/B/C/D tier system with correct pricing (A-list £18M, B-list £12M, C-list £7M, D-list £3M)
Celebrity cards show proper tier badges and buzz scores
Trending ticker displays celebrities with images and buzz scores
AI news generation working for celebrities
Budget tracking and updates working correctly
action_item_for_main_agent: CRITICAL: Fix profanity filter in team creation endpoint. The contains_banned_words() function is not properly rejecting offensive team names. Test with 'racist team' and 'nazi team' - both should return 400 error but currently return 200 success.
